name: Charles Calello
abstract: Charles Calello (born August 24, 1938) is an American arranger, composer, conductor, record producer, and singer born in Newark, New Jersey. Calello attended Newark Arts High School and the Manhattan School of Music, in New York City.

interviews.title: Charles Calello interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, July 21, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: David Spinozza
about_quotes.evidence: And it was Charlie Calello was producing Richie Havens... And I walked out of there just feeling like wow. We were all trying to do the right thing but Charlie wasn't happy
about_quotes.doc_title: David Spinozza interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Hartford, NY, September 27, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Anne Phillips
about_quotes.evidence: I was working with Charlie Calello who did all the Four Seasons stuff you know, and other stuff, and the record dates were well written, were good stuff and fun.
about_quotes.doc_title: Anne Phillips interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, February 24, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Alan Raph
about_quotes.evidence: There was one expression that Charlie Calello, who was one of the successful arrangers, used to use and that was, “Make it work.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Alan Raph interviewed by Monk Rowe, Danbury, Connecticut, November 20, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Warren Bernhardt
about_quotes.evidence: Charlie Calello was the arranger there... And I was a very good reader.
about_quotes.doc_title: Warren Bernhardt interviewed by Monk Rowe, Bearsville, New York, January 3, 2012
